headline: CAUTIOUS — selective BUY in OGDC + ATRL + PPL (quality value with yesterday's volume confirmation). 80% cash kept ahead of IMF May 15 (3 days). Avoid SEARL/TRG/PABC. Watch NBP for IMF positive print.
macro_lens: Brent crude back above $104 after Trump rejected Iran's peace counterproposal Sunday. Hormuz blockade extending — supply shock thesis intact for 1-2 weeks. PKR stable at 278. Policy rate 11.5% (SBP held). IMF mission lands May 15 — 3 days away. Phase-1 negative breadth (14% positive). Yesterday's dip was BOUGHT: KSE-100 closed -0.36% off its -1.4% intraday low. OGDC closed +0.25%, PPL closed +1.11% (the day's leader). ATRL was the only weak E&P -0.38%.
behavioural_lens: Yesterday: morning panic-sell into bid, midday absorption, afternoon consolidation, close near intraday high for OGDC/PPL. Volume profile = institutional accumulation in E&P leaders; retail-only in POL. Classic post-spike, dip-buy regime. Do not chase strength.
key_drivers: IMF mission May 15 (3 days) — biggest binary catalyst this week
Brent ~$105 (Hormuz blockade extending after Trump rejection Sun)
Yesterday dip-buy thesis validated: OGDC/PPL closed green
E&P momentum confirmed: PPL +1.11% on 3.27M volume (institutional)
ATRL is the model's #2 pick with unique MF-initiating signal
PKR stable at 278; SBP policy rate 11.5% (held)
Phase-1 still risk_off — wait for IMF outcome before deploying
key_risks: Iran de-escalation overnight => oil gap down, E&P sells off
Hawkish IMF leak in next 3 days => bank sector sells -1-2%
Brent retracement to <$103 => OGDC/PPL/MARI EPS cut risk
Cement demand still weak (LUCK/KOHC/DGKC/MLCF on the leash)
Thin liquidity (turnover narrow) — gap risk on bad headlines
narrative:
Tuesday May 12 opens with yesterday's playbook validated: the dip was absorbed, E&Ps closed flat-to-green despite KSE-100 -0.36%. The strategist's three model-approved BUYs (OGDC, ATRL, PPL) all behaved as expected — OGDC +0.25%, PPL +1.11% (star) on 3.27M volume (institutional accumulation), ATRL -0.38% (the only soft name; refining margin worry materialised intraday but did NOT break the thesis). POL closed essentially flat (+0.22%) — the HOLD verdict played out cleanly.

Going into Tuesday: Brent is still elevated at ~$105 (Hormuz blockade extending after Trump's Sunday rejection of Iran's counterproposal). IMF May 15 is now 3 trading days away — keep ~80% cash. The same three BUYs (OGDC 8%, ATRL 7%, PPL 5%) remain valid; ATRL is the highest-conviction pick the user hasn't entered yet (MF funds initiating — unique flow signal). If KSE-100 gaps up >+0.5% today, don't chase. If flat-to-red, add OGDC at 324-326, PPL at 230-231. AVOID list (SEARL/TRG/PABC) unchanged.

Risks: Iran de-escalation overnight (=> exit POL/PPL/MARI fast); hawkish IMF leak (=> NBP/MCB/UBL sell off); Brent retracement below $103 (=> trim E&P exposure).

Geopolitical Brent floor (~$105, Hormuz blockade) + IMF May 15 catalyst. Earnings in EROSION but value cushion is large. Strategic ANCHOR position.
